title: T-010 — Warehouse outbox event-type catalog (WH §5.1, §7.6, §11.4)
prompt:
# T-010 — Warehouse outbox event-type catalog (WH §5.1, §7.6, §11.4)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §5.1, §7.6, §11.4
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Define the 12 warehouse outbox event types as a Zod-validated catalog + TypeScript discriminated union so all services emit consistent payloads consumable by 04-PLANNING, 06-SCANNER, 12-REPORTING.

## Implementation contract
1. Create `packages/events/src/warehouse-events.ts` defining Zod schemas for: grn.completed, lp.reserved, lp.released, lp.consumed, lp.split, lp.merged, lp.scrapped, lp.blocked, lp.expired, lp.qa_changed, lp.output, location.created, location.updated.
2. Export discriminated-union `WarehouseEvent` keyed by `event` string.
3. Each schema includes `event`, `tenant_id`, `at`, plus event-specific fields per §7.6 / §11.4 examples.
4. Add Zod parse helper `parseWarehouseEvent(unknown): WarehouseEvent` used by outbox consumers in T-047 + 06-SCANNER.

## Files
- packages/outbox/src/event-types.ts [modify]
- packages/outbox/src/warehouse-events.ts [create]
- packages/outbox/src/__tests__/warehouse-events.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a service emits `lp.consumed` with the §11.4 payload shape, when validated by `parseWarehouseEvent`, then it passes.
2. Given an event with `event: 'lp.consumed'` is missing `wo_id`, when validated, then Zod rejects with a clear error path.
3. Given the catalog exports all 12 types, when a consumer adds a new `case` in a switch on `event`, then TypeScript exhaustiveness enforces handling all 12.

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/events/src/__tests__/warehouse-events.test.ts` asserting each event schema accepts the §-documented payload and rejects malformed ones.
- Run `pnpm --filter events test warehouse-events`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not emit events without `tenant_id` — multi-tenant consumers require it.
- Do not use string-typed event names in services — import from this catalog.
- Do not skip Zod validation in T-047 wiring.
